item.title: Illegal logging will sink government's timber processing plans
item.description: [Act Now PNG] Government plan to establish four regional centres for timber processing will fail unless it first puts an end to widespread illegal logging

item.title: Bougainville News Alerts: Independence statement from the President of Bougainville
item.description: [Bougainville News] My fellow Bougainvilleans, In the next few days, the resolve of the 97.8 percent of Bougainvilleans who voted for Bougainville to attain independence will be tested by the tabling of the Bi-Partisan Report on the 2019 Bougainville Referendum in the … Continue reading →

item.title: Innovation PNG 2026 Awards winners announced
item.description: [Business Advantage PNG] The winners of Papua Guinea’s major awards for innovation were announced at a special breakfast on 3 June at the Hilton Hotel in Port Moresby. The Innovation PNG 2026 Awards included prizes for corporate innovation, small businesses and young innovators. The post Innovation PNG 2026 Awards winners a

item.title: USP Council meeting reaffirms commitment to Pacific leadership and institutional renewal
item.description: [Education News PNG] The 101st meeting of the Council of The University of the South Pacific (USP) was convened in Nukuʻalofa in the Kingdom of Tonga on 21–22 The post USP Council meeting reaffirms commitment to Pacific leadership and institutional renewal first appeared on Education News PNG .

item.title: Morobe’s New Tourism Authority Board
item.description: [Inside PNG] Morobe Governor and Provincial Executive Council (PEC) Chairman, Rainbo Paita, has announced the appointment of a new Morobe Tourism Authority Board following a decision of the Provincial Executive Council during its Sam Sewe Meeting No. 04/2026 held on 23–24 April 2026. The decision formally revoke

item.title: PNG's Mt Hagen Airport Upgrade Paves Way for Boeing 737 and Airbus A220
item.description: [One Papua New Guinea] Air travellers in Papua New Guinea's Highlands Region can expect improved aviation services in the coming months as work gets underway to upgrade the runway at Mt Hagen's Kagamuga Airport. The National Airports Corporation has launched a large-scale rehabilitation program that will enable the airpor

item.title: Corporal and Capital Punishment in Papua New Guinea
item.description: [Pawa TV] Papua New Guinea has abolished capital punishment, replacing it with life imprisonment, while corporal punishment remains legally permitted in some contexts, highlighting ongoing tensions between tradition, law reform and human rights advocacy. The post Corporal and Capital Punishment in Papua New G

item.title: The Coolest Vanity Apps for You and Your Girls
item.description: [PNG Buzz] What’s possible in a week? If you dedicated seven days to the achievement of one goal, how ambitious could you make this goal? These were the questions that the multilingual friends Katy and Sara posed themselves when they determined to learn English in one week, to prove that it can be done and any

item.title: PNG's Kagamuga Airport Upgrade to Open Door for Modern Jet Services
item.description: [PNG Facts] Papua New Guinea's Mt Hagen Kagamuga Airport is set for a major transformation following the commencement of a runway rehabilitation project aimed at preparing the airport for larger and more modern aircraft by the end of 2026. The National Airports Corporation (NAC) announced that construction work

item.title: Who monitors Alluvial Mine operations: ESP Governor Bird
item.description: [Post Courier] With a lot of Alluvial Mines coming into operations in the country, East Sepik Governor Allan Bird has questioned the government on who is monitoring these Alluvial operations as there have been a lot of issues arising between the companies and locals of where the mines are operated. Governor Bird i

item.title: Cosmopolitan ordered to cancel Pride Night
item.description: [The PNG Bulletin] The Office of Censorship has issued a Stop Notice ordering The Cosmopolitan Night Club to immediately cancel its planned “Pride Night” event scheduled for today (Thursday, June 4, 2026). Chief Censor Jim Abani, acting under the Classification of Publication (Censorship) Act No. 18 of 1989, directed

item.title: NCDC, Bomana Detainees join forces in Major Port Moresby clean-up
item.description: [The PNG Sun] The National Capital District Commission (NCDC), in partnership with Bomana Correctional Services, has carried out a major community clean-up exercise in Port Moresby as part of ongoing efforts to improve urban cleanliness and support detainee rehabilitation. The initiative saw 30 detainees from Bom

item.title: Pisin mekim na balus bungim hevi long Goroka
item.description: [Wantok Niuspepa] Ol pisin i bin pasim ranwe long Goroka ples balus na i givim hevi long wanpela balus bilong PNG Air. Tupela wil bilong balus i bruk tasol no gat manmeri i kisim bagarap long dispela hevi.

item.title: Illegal logging will sink government’s downstream processing plans
item.description: [Act Now PNG] Government’s ambitious plan to establish four regional centres for timber processing and value-adding will fail unless it first puts an end to widespread illegal logging

item.title: Bougainville News Alerts :Bougainville Landowners demand mining companies halt reopening of copper pit
item.description: [Bougainville News] Traditional landowners are fighting back against plans to reopen an existing Bougainville copper mine which has remained dormant for nearly four decades. During a public event on Bougainville Island last week a warning was declared against the companies who landowners … Continue reading →

item.title: How Starlink and other LEO services can help unlock Papua New Guinea’s economic potential
item.description: [Business Advantage PNG] With the recent overturning of the National Court ban on Starlink operating in Papua New Guinea paving the way for NICTA approval, James Inglis, founder of NiuPay, shares how Low Earth Orbit connectivity can transform education, save lives and boost economic participation across the country. The pos

item.title: JICA and PNG Break Ground on Major Fisheries College Upgrade in Kavieng
item.description: [Education News PNG] The Government of Japan, through the Japan International Cooperation Agency (JICA), has joined the Government of Papua New Guinea and the National Fisheries Authority to The post JICA and PNG Break Ground on Major Fisheries College Upgrade in Kavieng first appeared on Education News PNG .

item.title: Brotherly love meets rivalry in M1 derby
item.description: [Inside PNG] Brothers Payne and Klese Haas clash in the M1 derby as Brisbane hosts Gold Coast. "He gives me a paragraph of stuff to work on," Klese says. The post Brotherly love meets rivalry in M1 derby first appeared on Inside PNG . The post Brotherly love meets rivalry in M1 derby appeared first on Inside PNG

item.title: PNG Eyes Economic Growth Through Major LNG and Mining Projects
item.description: [One Papua New Guinea] Papua New Guinea is preparing for what the Government describes as a long period of economic expansion as major resource projects move closer to development. Prime Minister James Marape says the Papua LNG project in Gulf Province and the Wafi-Golpu project in Morobe Province are among several large

item.title: The Police Tattoo
item.description: [Pawa TV] The Police Tattoo in Papua New Guinea celebrates policing tradition and cultural pride through band performances, strengthening regional cooperation and community trust, while marking a significant milestone in PNG's history. The post The Police Tattoo first appeared on PAWA TV .

item.title: PNG Education Reform Sparks Uncertainty Over National Exams in Pilot Provinces
item.description: [PNG Facts] Students, parents and teachers in Papua New Guinea’s pilot education reform provinces are still waiting for clear guidance on whether national examinations will proceed this year, with concerns now being raised on the floor of Parliament. PNG Education Reform Sparks Uncertainty Over National Exams i

item.title: Former PNG Kumul legend Darius Haili receives medical financial Aid
item.description: [Post Courier] The Nakanai District Administration has provided K5,000 in financial assistance to former Papua New Guinea Kumuls rugby league representative Darius Haili, from Kasia in the Hoskins local-level government area, to support his ongoing medical treatment. The post Former PNG Kumul legend Darius Haili r

item.title: Wewak MP raises questions over cyberattacks
item.description: [The PNG Bulletin] By GINNAH MINI Member for Wewak, Stanley Samban, has raised series of questions without notice in Parliament to the Minister for Information and Communication Technology over the abuse of social media platforms, specifically Facebook, by individuals operating under fake names and fraudulent accounts

item.title: Marimari Lutheran Church prepares to bid farewell to historic church after 52 years
item.description: [The PNG Sun] Tears, memories, thanksgiving and anticipation filled the air at Marimari Lutheran Church on Sunday as worshippers gathered for their final regular service inside one of Port Moresby’s most historic Lutheran churches. The church, established in 1974 and serving generations of Papua New Guineans for

item.description: [Education News PNG] Thirty-one cadets from the PNG Joint Forces Academy successfully completed a major field training exercise in Lae this week as part of their final military The post PNG Defence Cadets Complete Intensive Military Training Exercise in Lae first appeared on Education News PNG .

item.title: The Sacred Baining Fire Dance of East New Britain
item.description: [Pawa TV] The Baining people's Fire Dance in Papua New Guinea is a sacred night ritual, symbolising bravery and spiritual connection, marking significant life events and honouring ancestral spirits, while maintaining cultural identity. The acquisition sees Lees become Player 004 for the

item.title: Innovation PNG 2026 Awards winners announced
item.description: [Post Courier] A digital strata title platform designed to modernise land title management in PNG, a novel poultry feed solution powered by Black Soldier Fly larvae, and a student management system to support school teachers are the three winning innovations of the 2026 Innovation PNG Awards. item.title: PM Marape defends Constitutional Rights ahead of 2027 poll
item.description: [The PNG Sun] PNG Prime Minister James Marape has reaffirmed the government’s commitment to protecting the constitutional rights of candidates and voters ahead of the 2027 National General Election. PM Marape assured Parliament that preparations are underway to deliver a free, fair, safe and credible poll. Respon

item.title: Graun bruk katim Sepik Haiwe
item.description: [Wantok Niuspepa] Bikpela ren i mekim graun i bruk na bagarapim rot long Sepik Haiwe. Dispela rot i save bungim Is na Wes Sepik na i go olgeta long boda bilong PNG wantaim Indonesia.

The sponsorship sees BSP return as Diamond Sponsor for the exhibition that will bring together banks, property developer

item.title: The Court System Hierarchy in Papua New Guinea
item.description: [Pawa TV] Papua New Guinea's court system, rooted in colonial history, incorporates village, district, National, and Supreme Courts, blending customary law with modern legal principles to ensure fair justice nationwide. item.title: CEFI and The Salvation Army partner to advance Financial Inclusion across PNG
item.description: [Post Courier] The Centre for Excellence in Financial Inclusion (CEFI) and The Salvation Army (TSA) have formalised a new partnership through the signing of a Memorandum of Understanding (MoU) aimed at expanding financial literacy, financial inclusion and economic empowerment initiatives across Papua New Guinea. item.title: Deputy Opposition Leader petitions government
item.description: [The PNG Bulletin] By SEPKOLIN WALNE Deputy Opposition leader and Hiri-Koiari MP Keith Iduhu petitioned the government for fair representation and control over their 4.27% equity share within Kumul Petroleum Holdings Limited (KPHL). Iduhu emphasized that the governance of the 4.27% equity share at the Plant site on be

item.title: Minister Pala questioned over delayed K5 million for new District Headquarters
item.description: [The PNG Sun] Member for Middle Fly, Mai Maso Hewabi, has questioned Minister for National Planning and Monitoring, Sir Ano Pala, over delays in the release of K5 million allocated to each of seven newly created districts for the development of district headquarters. Raising the matter during Question Time in Par

item.title: Gavman Minista bam long kar long haiwe
item.description: [Wantok Niuspepa] Minista bilong 'Energy' na Memba bilong Kairuku, Peter Namea Isoaimo i bin bam long kar long 2nd Februeri, 2026 long Hiritano Haiwe. Em i bam long kar wantaim wanpela soldia bilong PNG Defence Force (PNGDF). Ol kar bilong tupela man i stap nau long polis stesin long 15 Mile na wok painimaut i gohet

item.description: [Bougainville News] The first permanent German presence in Kieta, Bougainville, began with the establishment of a Marist mission in 1901, followed by an official German colonial administrative office in 1905 to oversee the region. They would have seen, steaming into the deep natural harbour, … Continue reading →

item.title: PNG PM Defends NEC Decision on Kroton Shares

The Prime Minister was responding to accusations

item.title: The Power of the Kina
item.description: [Pawa TV] Over the past decade, Papua New Guinea's kina has faced significant depreciation due to reforms and global conditions, impacting purchasing power and increasing import costs, despite attempts to boost economic stability. item.title: Former Australian Officer Takes Senior PNG Police Role

item.title: A journey of faith, courage and compassion as PNG welcomes home baby Sawong
item.description: [Post Courier] There were tears, quiet smiles, and a profound sense of relief at Jacksons International Airport in Port Moresby yesterday (Wed 3rd June) as baby Sawong Kevin Mitian — one half of Papua New Guinea’s conjoined twins born last October — finally returned home after surgery and spending six months in Au

item.title: European Union deforestation regulation a component for PNG Agribusiness Exporters
item.description: [The PNG Bulletin] By Miriam Mandibi A regulatory requirement for compliance and certification for PNG Agribusiness Exporters into the European Markets discussed at the PNG EU Business Forum on the 3rd of June, 2026 in Port Moresby was the European Union Deforestation Regulation (EUDR). EUDR rule is to guarantee that

item.title: Man charged over alleged social media threats against Ok Tedi Operations
item.description: [The PNG Sun] A 34-year-old man has been arrested and charged for allegedly posting threats on social media calling for the closure of the Kiunga-Tabubil Highway and the disruption of operations linked to OK Tedi Mining Limited. Acting North Fly Provincial Police Commander Inspector Anjap Minata said the suspect

The presentation ceremony, attended by community leaders and police officers, marked the fourth vehicle donated by the district autho

item.title: One Tap Struggles To Serve Thousands
item.description: [Post Courier] BY NICK MUGUA MORE than 12,000 residents living in several communities in Tokarara are struggling to access clean drinking water, with only one functioning tap serving thousands of people in an area facing a growing water crisis. To access this post, you must purchase Web , Web – One-Day , Web – 14-

item.title: EU forum highlights export pathways for PNG
item.description: [The PNG Bulletin] By GINNAH MINI The 5th European Union–Papua New Guinea Business Forum has provided valuable insights to Small and Medium Enterprises (SMEs) on the requirements and opportunities for exporting agri-food products to European Union (EU) markets. The EU represents a premium market for Papua New Guinea.

item.title: Governor Rapa seeks clarity on Grade 8 and 10 examinations in Pilot Provinces
item.description: [The PNG Sun] Western Highlands Governor Wai Rapa has sought clarification from the Government on whether Grade 8 and Grade 10 students in five provinces selected for Papua New Guinea’s education reform pilot program will sit national examinations this year. Raising a question without notice during Parliament’s Q

Mog i pulap na wanem ol bodi i stap longtaim bai haus sik i bungim olgeta na planim long wanpela bi

item.description: [Act Now PNG] ACT NOW! is calls on the Forest Minster and National Forest Board to explain why controversial FCA logging permits have not been cancelled.

item.title: Carbon Trade in PNG

item.title: PM Marape defends constitutional rights of candidates and voters as PNG prepares for 2027 National Elections
item.description: [The PNG Bulletin] Prime Minister Hon. James Marape has assured Papua New Guineans that the Government remains fully committed to protecting Democratic Rights and ensuring a free, fair, safe, and credible National General Election in 2027. Responding during Question Time in Parliament today to Questions raised by More

item.title: Sir Puka raises Port Moresby General Hospital overcrowding concerns in Parliament
item.description: [The PNG Sun] Abau MP Sir Puka Temu has raised concerns in Parliament over severe overcrowding at Port Moresby General Hospital (PMGH), questioning whether the country’s largest hospital is being forced to perform the role of a regional referral facility due to mounting pressure on its services. During Question T

item.title: Australia pasim tingting bilong PNG wantaim ragbi lig
item.description: [Wantok Niuspepa] Australia i bilip ol i pasim tingting na bel bilong Papaua Niugini pinis taim ol i sapotim PNG long putim wanpela tim insait long NRL. Ol i bilip dispela i pasim PNG na i no wari long ol arapela wokbung PNG i gat wantaim ol arapela kantri olsem China.

item.title: Call on Forest Minister and Board to explain failure to cancel FCA permits
item.description: [Act Now PNG] ACT NOW is calling on the Forest Minster and National Forest Board to explain why controversial FCA logging permits have not been cancelled

item.title: Bougainville News Alerts :Rio Tinto urged to accelerate action on remediation of Panguna mine disaster, one year on from investigation
item.description: [Bougainville News] One year on from the release of an independent investigation into Rio Tinto’s former Panguna mine in Bougainville, communities living with the ongoing environmental impact are calling on the company to urgently move towards funding solutions, particularly in areas identified … Continue reading →

item.title: Hotel Review: Citi Boutique Hotel, Port Moresby
item.description: [Business Advantage PNG] Mary Tao reviews the hotel with 'something for everyone': the Citi Boutique Hotel, located adjacent to the Santos National Football Stadium in Port Moresby. The post Hotel Review: Citi Boutique Hotel, Port Moresby appeared first on Business Advantage PNG .

item.title: Australia Awards offers development grants for colleges
item.description: [Education News PNG] Teachers and nursing colleges in Papua New Guinea (PNG) have the opportunity to apply for Institutional Development Grants (IDG) up to K150,000.00 from Australia Awards. The post Australia Awards offers development grants for colleges first appeared on Education News PNG .

item.title: Kina Bank Backs Small Business Innovation for 2026
item.description: [Inside PNG] Kina Bank backs the 2026 Innovation PNG Awards, sponsoring the Small Business category to drive growth and recognize local creative entrepreneurs. The post Kina Bank Backs Small Business Innovation for 2026 first appeared on Inside PNG . The post Kina Bank Backs Small Business Innovation for 2026 ap

item.title: Fuel Price Drops in PNG Bringing Relief to Consumers Nationwide
item.description: [One Papua New Guinea] Motorists across Papua New Guinea are seeing relief at fuel stations after the Independent Consumer and Competition Commission (ICCC) confirmed that retail fuel prices have dropped following the National Government’s subsidy intervention. Fuel Price Drops in PNG Bringing Relief to Consumers Nationwi

item.title: Matrilineal & Patrilineal Societies in Papua New Guinea
item.description: [Pawa TV] Papua New Guinea's societal structures include matrilineal and patrilineal systems, influencing descent, land rights, and gender roles, with matrilineal communities empowering women through inheritance and social influence, and patrilineal focusing on male lineage. The post Matrilineal & Patrilineal

item.title: Different New Year Celebrations Around the World
item.description: [PNG Buzz] What’s possible in a week? If you dedicated seven days to the achievement of one goal, how ambitious could you make this goal? These were the questions that the multilingual friends Katy and Sara posed themselves when they determined to learn English in one week, to prove that it can be done and any

item.title: PNG Rugby Star Olam Brings Hope to Remote Chuave Communities
item.description: [PNG Facts] Rural communities in Chuave, Simbu Province received a boost over the weekend after PNG rugby league representative Justin Olam donated medical supplies valued at K30,000 to two local health centres. PNG Rugby Star Olam Brings Hope to Remote Chuave Communities/Photo supplied The assistance was deliv

item.title: Australia is helping to strengthen leadership across the transport sector in Papua New Guinea.
item.description: [Post Courier] 31 officials from Papua New Guinea’s transport agencies have graduated with certificates in leadership and management through an Australian Government-supported program. Participants from Papua New Guinea’s National Airports Corporation, Accident Investigation Commission, Civil Aviation Safety Autho

item.title: Rapa seeks clarity on Grade 10, 12 exams under 166 reform
item.description: [The PNG Bulletin] Western Highlands Governor Hon. Wai Rapa has raised concerns in Parliament over the rollout of the 166-education reform, with his province among five pilots implementing the changes to national examinations. He reminded Parliament that the government had publicly announced the phasing out of Grade 8

item.title: K25 million for Central Hospital to be released once project commences: Kapavore
item.description: [The PNG Sun] Health Minister Elias Kapavore has told Parliament that the K25 million allocated by the National Government for the proposed Central Provincial Hospital has not been released because the project has yet to commence. Responding to a supplementary question from Central Governor Rufina Peter during Qu

item.title: La Nina bai kam - gavman mas redi
item.description: [Wantok Niuspepa] National Weather Service (NWS) bilong PNG i tok 'La Nina' bai kam klostu nau tasol i no gat wanpela toksave i kam long gavman yet long wanem samting ol bai mekim o sapos ol i redi long wanem kain ol hevi dispela taim nogut bai nap long bringim.

item.title: K8 billion lost in illicit forestry activities under investigation
item.description: [Act Now PNG] Estimated K8.78 billion lost through illicit forestry activities and suspected money laundering schemes is now at the centre of four major investigations under Operation Arcadia, a multi-agency crackdown supported by INTERPOL.

item.title: Bougainville News Alerts : OBEC returns 45 writs, marks completion of 2025 ABG General Election
item.description: [Bougainville News] Bougainville Electoral Commissioner, Desmond Tsianai, today formally returned 45 writs comprising the Presidency, 38 single-member constituencies and the six reserved regional seats for women and Former Combatants. Tsianai formally handed the election writs to the outgoing Speaker, Simon Pentanu, of

item.title: On Starlink and Papua New Guinea’s innovative spirit
item.description: [Business Advantage PNG] The issuing of an operator licence to Starlink has generated headlines, but it is far from the only LEO connectivity provider in Papua New Guinea. Meanwhile, the shortlists for the 2026 Innovation PNG Awards have been revealed - and they include exciting small and medium businesses from around the c

item.title: Term 2 Delay in Manus
item.description: [Education News PNG] The Manus Provincial Education Board (PEB) has issued a delay notice for the start of term 2 due to severe weather. In a memorandum to The post Term 2 Delay in Manus first appeared on Education News PNG .

item.title: Kina Bank’s Rangeview Digital Hub Returns with Level 1 Upgrade
item.description: [Inside PNG] Kina Bank reopens its upgraded Rangeview Digital Hub on Level 1, bringing instant cards, loans, and digital support to Port Moresby shoppers. The post Kina Bank’s Rangeview Digital Hub Returns with Level 1 Upgrade first appeared on Inside PNG . The post Kina Bank’s Rangeview Digital Hub Returns with

item.title: PNG Rural Communities to Benefit from US Internet Funding
item.description: [PNG Facts] REMOTE communities across Papua New Guinea could soon have better internet access after the United States Government announced plans to provide US$30 million for satellite connectivity projects in the country. The funding will target essential services including schools, health facilities and disast

item.title: Wewak MP raise concerns of increasing fake social media accounts
item.description: [Post Courier] Member for Wewak Open Stanly Samban has raised a serious a question in Parliament today directed to the Minister for Information and Communication Technology in-regards to the escalating use of fake social media accounts to defame individuals and affect one’s character in the country. Mr Samban said

item.title: EIB Global provides loan to PNG to expand drinking water network
item.description: [The PNG Bulletin] EIB Global, the development arm of the European Investment Bank (EIB), is providing $25 million (approximately K95 million) in financing to PNG to expand networks for drinking water in towns across the country and in the capital Port Moresby. The project aims to bring steady supplies of potable wate

item.title: PM Marape commits to expanding Fisheries opportunities to Milne Bay
item.description: [The PNG Sun] Prime Minister James Marape has pledged to expand fisheries opportunities for Milne Bay Province, support greater local participation in the sector, and continue efforts to settle an outstanding K42 million owed to the province. Responding to questions raised by Milne Bay Governor Gordon Wesley duri

item.title: Government fails to answer UN concerns on illegal FCA logging and land abuses
item.description: [Act Now PNG] The Papua New Guinea government has failed to answer questions from the United Nations over the misuse of Forest Clearing Authority (FCA) logging licences and controversial land leases.

item.title: Bougainville News Alerts : Could tourism lead Bougainville into the future ?
item.description: [Bougainville News] Bougainville has gone through many turbulent periods over the past 100 years, not least the Civil War of the 1990s, which claimed as many as 20,000 lives. The government is now looking to develop the economy and has its eye … Continue reading →

item.title: People Moves: Digicel, CPL Group, Kina Bank, Remington Group, PNG FM, Dentons
item.description: [Business Advantage PNG] CEO steps down at Digicel PNG, new chair for CPL Group, new CFO at Kina Bank, first Solomon Islands country head for Remington Group, CEO appointed at PNG FM, new partner at Dentons. The post People Moves: Digicel, CPL Group, Kina Bank, Remington Group, PNG FM, Dentons appeared first on Business Adv

item.title: USP commits $1 million in student support amid global crisis
item.description: [Education News PNG] The University of the South Pacific (USP) has today announced a $F1 million student hardship and bursary support package to protect students and their families The post USP commits $1 million in student support amid global crisis first appeared on Education News PNG .

item.title: National Court Overturns Ombudsman Order Blocking Starlink Licence in PNG
item.description: [Inside PNG] PNG’s National Court quashes the Ombudsman Commission’s "ultra vires" order, clearing the legal path for NICTA to resume Starlink’s satellite license review. The post National Court Overturns Ombudsman Order Blocking Starlink Licence in PNG first appeared on Inside PNG . The post National Court Over

item.title: PNG Kumuls Hero Rhyse Martin Returns Home for Chiefs Role
item.description: [PNG Facts] PNG rugby league star Rhyse Martin will return home to help build the future of the PNG Chiefs after being appointed the club’s Football Operations Manager ahead of its planned entry into the NRL. The former PNG Kumuls captain is set to relocate to Port Moresby with his wife Vanessa and their two ch

item.title: Rural primary school in Kompiam Ambum District to host boxing matches
item.description: [Post Courier] The Enga Provincial Sports Council has identified Silim Primary School in the Kompiam Ambum District to host its upcoming boxing matches. The Enga Provincial Sports Council continued their district visits and inspected Silim Primary School as a potential venue for upcoming boxing matches. To access

item.title: CEPA hands over amended permit to TotalEnergies
item.description: [The PNG Bulletin] The Conservation and Environment Protection Authority (CEPA) has formally handed over the amended Upstream Level 3 (L3) Environmental Permit EP L3(1030) to TotalEnergies EP PNG Limited for the Papua LNG Project, marking a major step forward in the project’s development. The updated permit builds on

item.title: Secretary Leo urges neutrality among Provincial Monitoring Officers ahead of Election
item.description: [The PNG Sun] The Department of Provincial and Local Level Government Affairs (DPLLG) Secretary Philip Leo has urged newly appointed Senior Provincial Monitoring Officers to remain politically neutral and uphold professionalism as Papua New Guinea moves closer to the National General Election. Speaking during the

item.title: Government fails to answer UN concerns on illegal FCA logging and land abuses
item.description: [Act Now PNG] The Papua New Guinea government has failed to answer questions from the United Nations over the misuse of Forest Clearing Authority (FCA) logging licences and controversial SABL land leases.

item.title: Telikom PNG Expands Connectivity With Starlink Partnership
item.description: [PNG Facts] PNG telecommunications provider Telikom Limited says its new agreement with Starlink will help extend internet and digital services to more communities and businesses throughout the country. The company announced yesterday in Port Moresby that it has become an official authorised reseller for Starli

item.title: ACT NOW! Spotlights Illegal Logging at Asia Pacific Global Dialogue, Malaysia
item.description: [Act Now PNG] ACT NOW! recently attended and presented on illegal logging and forest crime in Papua New Guinea at the Asia- Pacific Regional Private Sector Dialogue meeting held in Malaysia.

item.title: Santos Cup Builds Stronger Future for PNG Women’s Rugby League
item.description: [PNG Facts] Women’s rugby league in Papua New Guinea is set for major improvements this year with PNG Rugby Football League introducing new standards and longer competition schedules for the 2026 Santos Cup season. Officials and team representatives gathered in Port Moresby from May 19 to 21 for the Santos Cup
